problem_statement: The top navigation bar uses hardcoded dark-navy colours (rgba(15,25,35,0.96) and #0f1923) in src/styles/nav.css. The client requests the nav be changed to a red that complements the existing brand palette (gold accent #c88a3e). The hardcoded values also diverge from the --bg-nav CSS variable defined in global.css, creating a design-token inconsistency.
solution_strategy: Adopt deep crimson #8B1A2A as the new nav background. Update --bg-nav in global.css (dark default, light theme, and OS-fallback blocks) to rgba(139,26,42,0.96). Replace every hardcoded navy value in nav.css with var(--bg-nav) or equivalent crimson rgba/hex literals. No new component or JS changes required.
entry_point: CSS-only change — visible on every page via the <nav id='main-nav'> element rendered by src/components/Nav.astro. Red background appears when scrolled past 40px (desktop), immediately on mobile, and in the full-screen mobile overlay.
user_story: As a website visitor, I want the top navigation bar to have a rich crimson-red background so that it visually stands out and reinforces the brand's bold mountain identity while harmonising with the gold accent elements throughout the page.

colour_choice.name: Deep Crimson
colour_choice.rationale: Evokes strength and mountain passion; pairs harmoniously with gold #c88a3e; deep/burgundy tone reads as premium rather than warning/error; white text exceeds WCAG AAA contrast on this background.

files_to_change.purpose: Update --bg-nav CSS variable to crimson in all three blocks: :root (dark default, line ~35), [data-theme='light'] (line ~67), and @media prefers-color-scheme:light :root:not([data-theme]) (line ~93). Nav is architecturally always-dark so crimson is correct in all theme states.

Replace three hardcoded navy colour values with crimson equivalents. Scrolled/force-dark rule now uses var(--bg-nav) for single-source-of-truth. Mobile overlay and mobile nav use inline crimson rgba/hex.

phases.name: Verify Current State (RED)
tasks: Run: npm run build && npm run preview
Confirm nav currently displays navy background on scroll
Confirm --bg-nav in global.css is rgba(15,25,35,0.96) — document current state

phases.name: Update CSS Design Token (GREEN — global.css)
tasks: In src/styles/global.css :root block (~line 35): change --bg-nav to rgba(139, 26, 42, 0.96)
In src/styles/global.css [data-theme='light'] block (~line 67): change --bg-nav to rgba(139, 26, 42, 0.96)
In src/styles/global.css @media prefers-color-scheme:light block (~line 93): change --bg-nav to rgba(139, 26, 42, 0.96)

phases.name: Update Nav Stylesheet (GREEN — nav.css)
tasks: In src/styles/nav.css .nav.scrolled / .nav.nav-force-dark (~line 14): replace 'background: rgba(15, 25, 35, 0.96)' with 'background: var(--bg-nav)'
In src/styles/nav.css @media (max-width: 768px) .nav (~line 176): replace 'background: rgba(15, 25, 35, 0.85)' with 'background: rgba(139, 26, 42, 0.90)'

phases.name: Quality & Validation
tasks: Run: npx astro check — must pass with zero errors
Run: npm run build — must complete without errors
Execute manual verification script: all 3 locales (/, /en/, /zh/), dark + light mode, desktop + mobile viewport
Confirm no hardcoded navy values remain in nav.css
Confirm --bg-nav token is consistent across all 3 blocks in global.css

design_decisions: Deep crimson #8B1A2A selected over brighter reds to maintain premium/prestige aesthetic consistent with mountain expedition brand
Nav is architecturally always-dark (comment in nav.css confirms this), so --bg-nav is set to crimson in ALL theme states (dark and light)
Light theme [data-theme='light'] --bg-nav updated from cream rgba(247,245,242,0.96) to crimson — nav text is already white and reads well on crimson at ~8.5:1 contrast ratio
Mobile overlay solid colour updated from #0f1923 to #8B1A2A for full consistency
Mobile semi-transparent nav uses 90% opacity (vs 96% for desktop) to maintain current pattern of slightly more transparency on mobile
Scrolled/force-dark rule now references var(--bg-nav) rather than hardcoded value — resolves design-token divergence
No JavaScript changes required — the scroll and hamburger logic in Nav.astro is colour-agnostic
No i18n changes required — colour is locale-independent
assumptions: The client wants crimson red specifically in the scrolled/sticky nav state — the transparent-at-top behaviour (no background visible when at page top) is preserved unchanged
Both dark and light theme modes should show the crimson nav (nav is always-dark by design)
No dark variant of the logo is required — the white/light logo already provides sufficient contrast on crimson
The gold CTA button (#c88a3e) accent colour remains unchanged
integration_requirements: No new Astro component imports required
No module registration or routing changes required
All changes are confined to two CSS files: src/styles/global.css and src/styles/nav.css
no_changes_required: src/components/Nav.astro — already applies correct CSS classes (.nav, .nav.scrolled, .nav.nav-force-dark, .nav-overlay)
Any page files (src/pages/**) — no changes needed
